---
title: "Analyzing US Births and Basketball Recruits"
author: "Alex Liu"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
```
```{r}
#| message: false
library(dplyr)

us_births <- read_excel("data/us_births_1994_2014.xlsx")
glimpse(us_births)
```
```{r}
us_births <- read_excel("data/us_births_1994_2014.xlsx")
summary(us_births[c("births", "year")])
```
```{r}
#| cache: true
births_tibble <- read_excel("data/us_births_1994_2014.xlsx") %>%
  mutate(day_of_week = factor(
    day_of_week,
    levels = c("Sun", "Mon", "Tues", "Wed", "Thurs", "Fri", "Sat"),
    ordered = TRUE
  ))
```

```{r}
#| label: avg_births
avg_births <- births_tibble %>%
  group_by(month, date_of_month) %>%
  summarize(avg_births = mean(births, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

print(head(avg_births))
```

```{r}
#| label: births_heatmap
# create a month name column that works whether `month` is numeric or already a name
avg_births_plot <- avg_births %>%
  mutate(month_chr = as.character(month)) %>%
  mutate(month_name = ifelse(!is.na(suppressWarnings(as.integer(month_chr))),
                             month.name[as.integer(month_chr)],
                             month_chr),
         month_name = factor(month_name, levels = rev(month.name)))

library(ggplot2)
ggplot(avg_births_plot, aes(x = date_of_month, y = month_name, fill = avg_births)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1,31,by=2)) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#ffffff", high = "#2166ac") +
  labs(x = "Date of month", y = "Month", fill = "Mean births") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| label: dec25_analysis
# For each year compute Dec 25 births, baseline mean of Dec 20-24 and 27-30, weekday of Dec 25, and percent
dec25_stats <- births_tibble %>%
  filter(month == 12, date_of_month >= 20, date_of_month <= 30) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(
    dec25 = sum(births * (date_of_month == 25), na.rm = TRUE),
    baseline = mean(births[date_of_month %in% c(20:24, 27:30)], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # attach weekday for Dec 25 from the original tibble
  left_join(
    births_tibble %>% filter(month == 12, date_of_month == 25) %>% select(year, day_of_week),
    by = "year"
  ) %>%
  mutate(pct_of_baseline = ifelse(is.na(baseline) | baseline == 0, NA_real_, dec25 / baseline * 100))

print(dec25_stats)
```

```{r}
#| label: christmas_summary
christmas_data <- dec25_stats
summary(christmas_data)
```

```{r}
#| label: dec25_trend
dec25_plot <- dec25_stats %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year))

ggplot(dec25_plot, aes(x = year, y = pct_of_baseline, color = day_of_week)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "#2c3e50") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(suffix = "%", accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Dec 25 as % of baseline", title = "Dec 25 births as percentage of surrounding baseline (colored by weekday)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#| label: births_model
# Fit linear model and print R-squared
births_model <- lm(births ~ factor(year) + factor(month) + day_of_week, data = births_tibble)
print(summary(births_model)$r.squared)
```

```{r}
#| label: births_adjusted
# Compute residuals relative to overall mean and attach as pct_resid
mean_births <- mean(births_tibble$births, na.rm = TRUE)
births_adjusted <- births_tibble %>%
  mutate(pct_resid = residuals(births_model) / mean_births * 100)

print(head(births_adjusted))
```

```{r}
#| label: calendar_resid
# Exclude Feb 29, compute mean pct_resid for each month/date, and assign 2001 anchor date
calendar_resid <- births_adjusted %>%
  filter(!(month == 2 & date_of_month == 29)) %>%
  group_by(month, date_of_month) %>%
  summarize(mean_pct_resid = mean(pct_resid, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(calendar_date = as.Date(sprintf("2001-%02d-%02d", month, date_of_month)))

print(head(calendar_resid))
```

```{r}
#| label: calendar_plot
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# compute Thanksgiving for 2001 (4th Thursday of November)
thurs_nov <- seq.Date(as.Date('2001-11-01'), as.Date('2001-11-30'), by = 'day')
thurs <- thurs_nov[weekdays(thurs_nov) == 'Thursday']
thanksgiving2001 <- thurs[4]

holiday_name <- function(d) {
  sd <- format(d, "%m-%d")
  if (sd == "12-25") return("Christmas")
  if (sd == "01-01") return("New Year's Day")
  if (sd == "07-04") return("Independence Day")
  if (d == thanksgiving2001) return("Thanksgiving")
  return(NA_character_)
}

annot <- calendar_resid %>% arrange(mean_pct_resid) %>% slice(1:5) %>%
  mutate(label = coalesce(sapply(calendar_date, holiday_name), format(calendar_date, "%b %d"))) %>%
  slice(1:3)

ggplot(calendar_resid, aes(x = calendar_date, y = mean_pct_resid)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2c3e50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_point(data = annot, aes(x = calendar_date, y = mean_pct_resid), color = "red", size = 2) +
  geom_text(data = annot, aes(x = calendar_date, y = mean_pct_resid, label = label), vjust = -1, size = 3) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %d", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(x = "Calendar date (2001 anchor)", y = "Mean % residual (pct_resid)", title = "Average percent residual by calendar date — holiday dips annotated") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
